events.title: National Dental Care - business assets comprising the Comfy Dental clinic, Mandurah, WA - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
NDC HoldCo Pty Ltd (together with its subsidiaries, NDC), through its
wholly owned subsidiary, NDC Australia Pty Limited, proposes to
acquire from the Comfy Dental Trust 100% of the business assets
comprising the Comfy Dental Clinic (Comfy Dental) (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, NDC, is a dental services organisation that operates a
network of dental clinics that provide a range of general dentistry
services. NDC has dental clinics in all Australian states and
territories except Tasmania. It has one existing dental clinic located
in Mandurah, WA (DB Dental Mandurah). NDC is majority owned by
funds managed by Crescent Capital Partners.
The target, Comfy Dental is a dental clinic located in Mandurah, WA
that provides a range of general dentistry services.
The parties overlap in the supply of general dentistry services.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The aggregation in the supply of general dentistry services in the
local area arising from the Acquisition is not significant. This
assessment is not impacted by consideration of previous
acquisitions by NDC in the last three years.
• NDC will continue to face competition from a number of
alternative general dental clinics in the local area surrounding
the target.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

deals_cleared.merger_name: Enverus – Spatial Business Systems
deals_cleared.merger_description: Enverus, Inc. (**Enverus**), a Texas corporation, proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of SBS Optionco, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and SBS Blocker, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (collectively, with each of their direct and indirect subsidiaries, **Spatial Business Systems**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Enverus, Inc. (Enverus) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital of SBS
Optionco, LLC and SBS Blocker, LLC (collectively, with each of their
direct and indirect subsidiaries, Spatial Business Systems), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Enverus, is a global Software-as-a-Service and data
provider to the energy industry. Enverus was founded in 1999 and is
headquartered in Austin, Texas. Enverus offers a platform that
integrates analytics, supply chain management, and commercial
intelligence tools for oil and gas, power, utilities and renewable
energy customers.
Enverus is controlled by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates
of Blackstone, Inc. (together with its affiliates or affiliated investment
funds, Blackstone). Blackstone, Inc. is a US-based corporation,
incorporated under the laws of Delaware, United States.
The target, Spatial Business Systems, is a global business
headquartered in Littleton, Colorado in the United States. Spatial
Business Systems supplies design software for utilities and critical
network infrastructure assets to customers across electric and gas
utilities, engineering and construction firms, and broadband
infrastructure operators. Spatial Business Systems’ software
solutions are designed to automate and streamline engineering and
design workflows, integrate spatial and design data, and improve
design effectiveness. In Australia, Spatial Business Systems
provides distribution design and substation design services to
customers.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap, and limited potential vertical
relationships, between Enverus or Blackstone’s controlled
portfolio companies and Spatial Business Systems in the
supply of software for designing electric and gas distribution
networks and substations to customers in Australia
b. Spatial Business Systems has a small presence in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Wentworth Inn OpCo I Pty Ltd

deals_cleared.merger_description: Wentworth Capital Investment Management Pty Ltd (together with its holding companies and subsidiaries, **Wentworth**) is an Australian asset manager based in Sydney which specialises in real estate. Its core activities are asset management, real estate private equity and real estate private credit.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Wentworth Inn Prop TC Pty Ltd, Wentworth Inn OpCo I Pty Ltd and
Wentworth Inn OpCo II Pty Ltd (the Acquirers) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of their proposed acquisition of:
• 100% of the hotel businesses and related assets of Novotel
Darling Harbour and Ibis Darling Harbour (Hotel Businesses)
from Pearl Hotels D 1993 Pty Limited
• the leasehold interests in the land on which the Hotel Businesses
operate (the Property) from The Trust Company (Australia)
Limited in its capacity as trustee of the Pearl Hotels ZC 2013
Trust
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The Acquirers are funds managed by Wentworth Capital Investment
Management Pty Ltd (together with its holding companies and
subsidiaries, Wentworth). Wentworth is an Australian asset manager
based in Sydney which specialises in real estate. Its core activities
are asset management, real estate private equity and real estate
private credit.
The Hotel Businesses offer hotel accommodation services and
ancillary services related to the operation of a hotel under brands
associated with the Accor Group (which will continue to manage the
hotels post-Acquisition).
Wentworth states that it does not hold any interests in hotel or other
short-term accommodation businesses in Australia. However, one
non-controlling co-investor in the Acquirers, Mulpha Australia
Limited (which is ultimately owned by Mulpha International Bhd
(together, Mulpha)), has interests in the InterContinental Sydney at
Circular Quay and a proposed venue in Sussex Street, Sydney.
Mulpha is an international real estate and hospitality investor
headquartered in Malaysia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
• there appears to be no, or a very limited, horizontal overlap
between Wentworth and the Hotel Businesses
• there are alternative suppliers of short-term accommodation and
ancillary services in Sydney, including in Darling Harbour
• the Accor Group will continue to operate and manage the Hotel
Businesses post-Acquisition.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Ochre Health Pty Limited (**Ochre Health**) proposes to acquire the business and all of the assets of the Telegraph Road Clinic (**TRC**) in Bracken Ridge, Queensland, through a business and assets acquisition agreement (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Ochre Health Pty Limited (Ochre Health) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the business and all
of the assets of Telegraph Road Clinic (TRC) in Bracken Ridge,
Queensland, as described in the transaction documents provided as
part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide
general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban
communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 68
medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW,
Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria. Ochre Health Group’s medical
recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals
and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place
locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.
Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital
(Genesis), an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in
businesses operating in the healthcare sector. Genesis’s
investments also include Holdsworth House Medical Group
(Holdsworth House), which operates a general medical practice in
Fortitude Valley, Brisbane and another general medical practice in
Sydney which is co-located with a medical research centre, specialist
medical clinic and dentistry clinic.
The target, TRC, is a family medical practice providing
comprehensive general practice medical care from its clinic located
in the Bracken Ridge Plaza, Shop 26, 250 Telegraph Road, Bracken
Ridge, Queensland, and home visits to nearby patients (within a 3km
radius of the clinic). TRC is owned and operated by Telegraph Road
Medical Services Pty Ltd as trustee for Telegraph Road Medical
Services Unit Trust.
Ochre Health, Genesis and TRC supply general practice medical
services in Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no overlap in the supply of general practice medical
services in the local Bracken Ridge area in Queensland and a
small horizontal overlap between Ochre Health, Genesis and
TRC in the broader Brisbane area
b. there are other clinics supplying general practice medical
services near Bracken Ridge, Queensland.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_description: BGIS Pty Ltd (**BGIS**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in the Ascot Air Facilities Pty Ltd (**Ascot Air**) from its shareholders (the **Acquisition**).

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
BGIS Pty Ltd (BGIS) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Ascot Air
Facilities Pty Ltd (Ascot Air), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, BGIS, is a national provider of integrated facilities
management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air
conditioning (HVAC) services largely as part of broader facilities
management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through
internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.
The target, Ascot Air, is a provider of commercial HVAC and
mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and
construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation
ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air
conditioning systems, building management systems and controls,
drafting and design support and commissioning services. Ascot Air
provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane,
Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.
Both parties supply commercial HVAC and related mechanical
services within South East Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between BGIS and Ascot
Air in the supply of commercial HVAC and related
mechanical services to areas within South East Queensland
b. while there is a potential vertical supply relationship between
the parties, there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or
other concerning vertical effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct post-acquisition
c. there are alternative suppliers of commercial HVAC and
related mechanical services in areas within South East
Queensland.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.

effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

deals_cleared.merger_name: CVC Capital Partners – The animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group
deals_cleared.merger_description: The Acquisition is a private equity financial investment concerning the acquisition of DSM-Firmenich AG’s Animal Nutrition & Health business (the **Target**) by the following newly formed companies:

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CVC Capital Partners plc. (CVC Capital Partners) proposes to
acquire the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-
Firmenich Group as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition consists of:
• CVC Capital Partners to indirectly acquire, through Specialty
Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco B.V.,
and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., an 80% shareholding and
sole control over the SpecialtyCo Business and EssentialCo
Business (which together comprise the animal nutrition and
health division), and
• the DSM-Firmenich Group to (indirectly through to be
incorporated entities) reinvest in each of the SpecialityCo
Business and the EssentialCo Business to retain a ~20%
non-controlling, minority stake in each.
Specialty Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco
B.V., and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc applied for a notification waiver
in respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, CVC Capital Partners, is a global alternative investment
manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure and
secondaries. CVC Capital Partners hold interests in companies
active in a variety of industries around the world, including in
financial services, chemicals, utilities, manufacturing, retailing and
distribution, primarily in Europe, the US and the Asia-Pacific region.
The target, the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-
Firmenich Group, is a global provider of science-based animal
nutrition and health solutions. Its core business is the manufacture
and supply of animal nutrition ingredients, particularly vitamins and
carotenoids, for livestock and aquaculture feed. It is also involved in
the premix industry, which serve as a link from feed additives to final
feed composition in the animal nutrition value chain.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap
between the products and services supplied by the animal nutrition
and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group and CVC Capital
Partners controlled portfolio companies in Australia, or any vertical
relationships.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_name: Austral Resources Australia – Lady Loretta mine
deals_cleared.merger_description: On 16 February 2026, Austral Resources Australia Ltd (**Austral**) entered into an agreement with Noranda Pacific No. 2 Pty Ltd (**NP2**) and Mount Isa Mines Limited (**MIM**) (both indirect subsidiaries of Glencore plc) to acquire:

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Austral Resources Australia Limited (Austral) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:
• 100% of the shares in Noranda Pacific Pty Ltd (NPPL), the
owner and operator of the Lady Loretta zinc mine in
Queensland, from Noranda Pacific No. 2 Pty Ltd (NP2) and
Mount Isa Mines Limited (MIM); and
• the exploration permits associated with the Lady Loretta
mine from MIM,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Austral, is an Australian-based copper producer
headquartered in Queensland. Austral owns and operates a number
of copper mines in Australia, including the Lady Annie copper mine
which is located 16km south of the Lady Loretta mine.
The Lady Loretta mine ceased production in 2025. Prior to its
closure, the mine produced zinc concentrate (with lead and silver
concentrate by-products), 100% of which was supplied to related
entities of NPPL for processing in Mount Isa.
NPPL, NP2 and MIM are indirect wholly-owned subsidiaries of
Glencore plc, a multi-national commodity trading and mining
company headquartered in Switzerland.
Following completion, Austral will assume the progressive
rehabilitation obligations of the Lady Loretta mine, and will enter into
an offtake agreement with Glencore in respect of future copper
products derived from the Lady Loretta tenements.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information before it, the ACCC considers that the
Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. Austral’s share of the actual and expected production and
supply of copper concentrate and zinc concentrate in
Australia and globally is expected to be low
b. there are several alternative producers and suppliers of
copper concentrate and zinc concentrate in Australia and
globally.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

determination_table_content.details:
L701 Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposal to enter into an option to lease agreement with the
Western Australian Land Authority (trading as DevelopmentWA) over
part of Lot 701 within the Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area in
Western Australia, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). The option
provides L701 Pty Ltd with the right to lease the land following
completion of development milestones.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, L701 Pty Ltd, is a subsidiary of Element Zero Pty Ltd
(Element Zero), an Australian company developing technology and
projects for the production of low-emissions mineral products,
including green iron. Element Zero develops technology and projects
for the production of low-carbon mineral products and intends to
develop a commercial-scale green iron production facility.
DevelopmentWA is the Western Australian Government’s land and
development agency responsible for facilitating industrial land
development. DevelopmentWA develops and manages industrial
land and infrastructure in Western Australia, including within the
Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area.
If the option for lease is exercised, the parties intend to enter into a
long-term industrial ground lease to enable development and
operation of a green iron production facility.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal competitive overlap
b. there appear to be alternative sites available for Element

c. the Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or
prevent competition from rivals.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.

object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.

deals_cleared.merger_description: Henkel AG & Co. KGaA, through its subsidiary Henkel Global Supply Chain B.V. (**Henkel**), proposes to acquire all the shares in and sole control of Stahl Parent B.V. (together with subsidiaries, **Stahl**) from Stahl Group S.A., which is indirectly controlled by affiliates of Wendel S.E. (**Wendel**).

determination_table_content.details:
Henkel Global Supply Chain B.V. (Henkel), which is a subsidiary of
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA, applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of all of the share capital in Stahl Parent
B.V. (together with its subsidiaries, Stahl), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Henkel, is a German public company listed on the
Frankfurt Stock Exchange, headquartered in Düsseldorf. It is active
worldwide through its Adhesive Technologies business and
Consumer Brands business. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies
business is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional
coatings for industrial customers, consumers and craftsmen. Its
Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite,
Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt.
Henkel’s Consumer Brands business offers a portfolio of consumer
brands with a focus on Laundry & Home Care and Hair Care. Its
consumer brands include Persil, Schwarzkopf, Perwoll, Syoss, Somat
and Bref.
The target, Stahl, is a manufacturer of specialty coatings for flexible
materials headquartered in the Netherlands. With subsidiaries in
Europe, Asia, and the Americas, the company is active worldwide. Its
portfolio comprises leather finishing coatings, performance coatings,
and packaging coatings, serving customers mainly in mobility
(automotive and aviation interiors), packaging, fashion & lifestyle,
and home furnishing.
In Australia, the parties both supply barrier (interior) coatings for
paper packaging and laminating adhesives for flexible packaging to
industrial customers, which are subsets of packaging coatings and
packaging adhesives respectively.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
• there is a small horizontal overlap between the parties in the
supply of packaging coatings and packaging adhesives
• the parties combined market shares in the supply of packaging
coatings and packaging adhesives are estimated to be low
• there are alternative providers of packaging coatings and
packaging adhesives in Australia, including in the supply of the
subsets of these products in which the parties overlap
• there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or other concerning
vertical or conglomerate effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct, bundling or tying of products post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

deals_cleared.merger_name: MidOcean Energy – JERA Gorgon
deals_cleared.merger_description: MidOcean Energy proposes to indirectly acquire a:

events.title: Questionnaire - MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon Pty Ltd
events.display_title: Questionnaire - MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon Pty Ltd

events.title: MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon - Phase 1 determination
events.display_title: MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
MidOcean Energy proposes to indirectly acquire a:
• ~0.417% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project located on
Barrow Island, Western Australia, and
• ~0.735% interest in the Ichthys LNG Project located in the
Browse Basin in offshore Western Australia, including in the
exploration activities of the expired WA-285-P tenement
via the acquisition by a newly incorporated subsidiary, MidOcean
Australia Holdings 2 Pty Ltd (MidOcean Holdings) of 100% of the
shares in JERA Gorgon Pty Ltd (JERA Gorgon) from JERA Australia
Pty Ltd (JERA Australia), pursuant to a share sale agreement (the
Acquisition).
The Acquisition has given rise to a pre-empt right in favour of the
other joint venture participants to the Ichthys LNG Project. As a
result, one of the Ichthys joint venture participants has exercised its
right to acquire the underlying Ichthys LNG Project interests held
indirectly by JERA Gorgon. This subsequent acquisition would occur
following completion of the Acquisition and as such, has not been
considered as part of this determination.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

MidOcean Energy
The acquirer, MidOcean Holdings, is part of MidOcean Energy, which
is a liquefied natural gas (LNG) company that supplies natural gas in
Australia and LNG and condensate overseas. It holds minority
interests in LNG projects located in Western Australia (WA) and
Queensland, including a:
• 1% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project: a three train LNG
facility and domestic gas plant on Barrow Island, WA,
• 5% interest in the Pluto LNG Project: a one train LNG project
on the Burrup Peninsula, WA, and
• 1.25% interest in the QCLNG Project: a two-train onshore
coal seam gas project on Curtis Island, Queensland.
MidOcean Energy is managed by EIG Management Company, LLC
and its affiliates, a US institutional investor that specialises in
investments in energy and energy-related infrastructure globally.
JERA Gorgon
The target, JERA Gorgon, is a subsidiary of JERA Australia, which in
turn is owned by a Japanese-incorporated power generation
company. JERA Gorgon supplies natural gas in Australia and LNG,
liquefied petroleum gas and condensate overseas. Its interests in
Australia includes:
• a ~0.417% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project, and
• 100% of the shares in JERA Ichthys Pty Ltd, which in turn
holds ~0.735% interest in the Ichthys LNG Project and its
associated exploration permit. The Ichthys LNG Project
extracts gas in WA which is transported via pipeline to a
two-train LNG facility in the Northern Territory.

MidOcean Energy and JERA Gorgon (the Parties) horizontally
overlap in the production and supply of natural gas in WA.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Acquisition would result in a very small change in MidOcean
Energy’s shareholding in the Gorgon LNG Project that would not
materially change MidOcean Energy’s influence or control over
the project.
• The Acquisition would provide MidOcean Energy with a very
small shareholding in the Ichthys LNG Project that would not
confer control or influence over the project.

Energy’s share of gas production in WA. MidOcean Energy would
remain a very small supplier of domestic gas in WA and continue
to face competition from a range of other suppliers.

dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any

Tribunal.

The acquirer, IBWM Pty Ltd, is a subsidiary of Ironbark. Ironbark has

deals_cleared.merger_name: Coles – supermarket site in New Norfolk (TAS)
deals_cleared.merger_description: Coles Supermarkets Australia Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Coles Group Limited (**Coles**), has signed a letter of offer with Tipalea Private No.31 Pty Ltd (ACN 686 197 115) to facilitate Coles leasing a new standalone supermarket to be developed at 120 Glebe Road, New Norfolk, Tasmania 7140 (the **Property**).

events.title: Questionnaire - Coles - supermarket site in New Norfolk (TAS)

events.title: Coles New Norfolk - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Coles Supermarkets Australia Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of Coles Group
Limited (Coles), proposes to acquire a leasehold interest at 120
Glebe Road, New Norfolk, Tasmania 7140 (the Target Site) to
establish a new standalone supermarket site from Tipalea Private
No.31 Pty Ltd (Tipalea) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Coles, is an Australian public company (ASX:COL) that
primarily operates:
• supermarkets nationwide, and
• retail liquor stores nationwide, trading under the Liquorland,
Liquorland Warehouse and Liquorland Cellars brands.
The vendor, Tipalea, is an Australian private company specialising in
property development and investment in New South Wales, Victoria,
Queensland and Tasmania.
The Target Site is located in New Norfolk, Tasmania, approximately
35km northwest of Hobart. The Target Site is currently occupied by a
nursery garden and supplies shop.

The leasehold interest would be an input to Coles’ supermarket
retailing operations.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, other information provided by the parties and
publicly available sources.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• Pursuant to the Acquisition, Coles proposes to open a new
supermarket site in New Norfolk. Coles does not currently
operate in New Norfolk or the surrounding areas. The closest
existing Coles supermarket at Bridgewater is 18.5km driving
distance from the Target Site.
• The Acquisition would introduce a competing grocery retailer to
the existing full line Woolworths supermarket and the smaller
IGA X-Press in New Norfolk.
• The Target Site is not currently used for grocery retailing.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
